title: Youth Residential Support Worker
description:
Are you looking for a flexible role where you can make a real impact on the lives of young people in care?

Purpose of the Job

As a Children's mainstream Residential Support Worker, you'll provide essential support to young people aged 12-17 living in mainstream residential homes. Your role is to offer stability, promote independence, and create a safe, nurturing environment where young people can thrive.
This is ad-hoc, temporary work,
ideal for those seeking flexibility while still wanting to make a meaningful difference.

Key Details of the Job

- Location:
various locations in Leeds

- Pay:
£14.82 per hour + uplifts for waking nights and weekends

- Contract:
Ad-hoc temporary work (no guaranteed hours)

- Shifts:
Earlies, lates, sleep-ins, waking nights

Requirements

- Ligature Training (mandatory)

- Minimum 1 year experience
in residential childcare, youth support, safeguarding, or a similar environment

- Strong understanding of safeguarding, risk management, and de-escalation

- Experience engaging positively with young people who may display challenging behaviours

Desirable

- Experience in Mental Health Secure settings

- Additional behavioural management or risk-reduction training

Apply Today

If you're ready to support young people and work flexibly around your lifestyle, we'd love to hear from you.
summary: Youth Residential Support Worker role in Leeds focused on support for children and young people.

title: Behaviour Support Worker
company: Veritas Education Recruitment Ltd • Agency • Contract
description:
Job: Behaviour Support Worker
Type: Long Term Contract
Start Date: April 2026
Full Time - Leeds

Veritas Education are working with an alternative provision within the Leeds area who are looking for an experienced Behaviour Support Worker, to start from after the Easter holidays in April 2026 until the end of the academic year.
The provision supports children from 11-16 years old, who are currently on temporary exclusion, or facing a fixed permanent exclusion from mainstream schooling. The provision is set up with fantastic facilities, which enables students to develop academically, socially and emotionally, and helps facilitate their transition back into either mainstream schools or specialist provisions.
The role of a behaviour support worker will be to support key children on a 1:1 basis across their learning, working with them during their lessons, help them learn self-regulation and emotional control, but also manage behaviour and expectations across the setting.
The ideal staff member will be someone who is experienced in supporting SEMH/Behavioural needs, confident working with children on a one-to-one basis, and pro-active when it comes to getting involved within the student's core and vocational lessons to help create an engaging and inclusive learning environment!
Veritas Education can also offer essential training for this role such as Level 2 Team Teach training, to ensure you know the required behaviour management and de-escalation skills to support learners in crisis.
Please contact George Walton at
summary: Behaviour Support Worker role in Leeds focused on behaviour support in an education setting.

title: Support Worker
description:
Are you good with people? Would you like to use your skills to support individuals to get into meaningful employment? We have a unique role working as a Support Worker in our Courtyard Apartments.

At Courtyard Services Horsforth, Leeds you will support people with learning disabilities, encouraging and empowering them to develop skills and confidence in a warm and vibrant setting.

We need calm, resourceful and energetic individuals who are highly motivated, hardworking and keen to support older people in a proactive and holistic manner. Your job role will involve:

- Helping service users to engage in a wide range of exciting social activities both within and outside their homes,

- Developing warm and trusting relationships with service users and their families,

- Enabling service users to access local services,

- Encouraging service users to move towards independent living,

- Support to meet the personal care and support needs of service users in a way that respects the dignity of the individual and promotes independence.

This role includes a range of shifts including daytime, evenings and sleeping-in duties.
Previous experience is essential for our relief roles. Vacancy Reference Number: 84992
Applications for this role must be submitted via the Creative Support website using the above vacancy reference number

Full training is provided, as is the opportunity to work towards QCF Diploma in Health and Social Care.
Benefits of working with Creative Support:

- Free 24/7 counselling and employee support programme,

- Support to complete the nationally recognised Care Certificate and Social Care Diploma,

- Choice of weekly or monthly pay,

- A one-off bonus of £100 upon successful completion of the 4-month probationary period,

- Pension contributions,

- 28 days Annual Leave and company paid enhanced DBS.
summary: Support Worker role in Leeds focused on support for adults with learning disabilities.

title: Support Worker
description:
NO WORK SPONSORSHIPS OFFERED

About Us:The Cambian Group are a leading provider of residential care for children and young people. We are dedicated to creating a safe, nurturing environment where children can thrive and develop their full potential. Our residential services are designed to meet the needs of young people who require additional support due to complex emotional, behavioural, or social challenges. We are looking for passionate, committed individuals to join our team as Residential Support Workers.
Role Overview:As a Residential Support Worker in our Children's Services, you will play a pivotal role in helping children and young people lead fulfilling, positive lives. You will be responsible for providing high-quality care, support, and guidance, ensuring the well-being and safety of the children and young people in our care. You will work closely with a team of professionals to create and implement individualised care plans that support their development, emotional needs, and educational goals.
Key Responsibilities:
Support children and young people with daily living activities, including personal care, education, and recreational activities.Encourage and promote positive behaviour, helping children manage and overcome emotional or behavioural difficulties.Build trusting relationships with young people, fostering a safe and supportive environment.Assist in the implementation of care plans, ensuring individual needs are met.Monitor and report on the progress, behaviour, and welfare of young people, maintaining accurate records.Work collaboratively with colleagues, social workers, schools, and external agencies to provide holistic care.Participate in team meetings, training, and supervisions to ensure the highest standards of care are maintained.Help create a positive and inclusive atmosphere within the home, contributing to a stable and nurturing environment.
What We Are Looking For:
A genuine passion for working with children and young people and a desire to make a difference.Previous experience in a care or support role, ideally within residential childcare or children's services, is advantageous but not essential.Strong communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to build rapport with young people.Resilience, patience, and the ability to remain calm under pressure.A commitment to safeguarding and promoting the welfare of children and young people.A Level 3 qualification in Children and Young People's Workforce, Health and Social Care, or equivalent is desirable, but not essential (training will be provided).A full UK driving licence is desirable.
What We Offer:
A rewarding career where you can make a tangible difference in the lives of children and young people.Comprehensive induction and ongoing training and development opportunities.Supportive team environment with regular supervisions and opportunities for career progression.Competitive salary and benefits package.Pension scheme and employee assistance programme.Flexible working patterns to support work-life balance.
Additional Information:
The role may involve shift work, including evenings, weekends, and sleep-in duties, depending on the needs of the home.All successful candidates will be subject to an enhanced DBS check and other relevant safeguarding checks.The Cambian Group is committed to safeguarding and promoting the welfare of children and young people. All appointments will be subject to comprehensive pre-employment checks.
How to Apply:
If you are passionate about making a difference in the lives of children and young people and meet the criteria above, we would love to hear from you. Please submit your application outlining your experience and motivation for applying.
Join us at The Cambian Group and be part of a team that is changing lives every day.
INDRESMP
summary: Support Worker role in Bradford focused on support for children and young people.

title: Support Worker Driver
description:
Creative Support are looking for warm, reliable and proactive Support Workers to join our team in Bradford to provide quality care and support to individuals with mental health needs.

Your role will include:

- Developing warm and trusting relationships with service users, encouraging them to express their needs, views and concerns

- Working in partnership with service users through the recovery process, being flexible and responsive to the individual needs of the service user.

- Providing practical support with household tasks and personal care,

- Contributing to the provision of a comfortable and safe environment,

- Escorting service users to access health services, educational opportunities, leisure and social activities.

You should have a full UK/European driving licence and the willingness to drive the company vehicle.
You will be paid on a weekly basis.
Vacancy Reference Number: 82416
Applications for this role must be submitted via the Creative Support website using the above vacancy reference number.

What we offer:

- Comprehensive induction and commitment to ongoing learning and development

- 28 days annual leave,

- Company paid enhanced DBS for all staff

- Free employee support programme

- All our staff are supported 24/7 by our out-of-hours teams

- Support to complete the nationally recognised Care Certificate and Social Care Diploma.

We are a passionate, inclusive and anti-racist organisation. We are a Stonewall Diversity Champion and a Disability Confident Employer. We are fully committed to being a welcoming and very inclusive employer of choice.
summary: Support Worker Driver role in Bradford focused on mental health support with driving duties.

title: Recovery Support Worker
description:
Are you looking for a rewarding career that is stimulating and varied each day?

Creative Support are looking for warm, reliable and proactive Support Workers to join our Floating Staff team in Bradford to provide quality care and support to individuals with mental health needs. We work across the city in four local teams, ensuring staff offer personalised mental health recovery support, encouragement and practical support to service users.
As a Recovery Support Worker you will provide support to people with mental health support needs in a range of supported accommodation and community support services, including support to some customers with behaviours which challenge.

You will be a highly motivated person with the mature ability to work on your own initiative and a positive approach to person-centred care. You must be an excellent communicator and have skills in positive communication and engagement and will be able to demonstrate unconditional positive regard for all our service users. You must have at least twelve months experience of working with people with mental health support needs. We are looking for people who are flexible to work on a rota basis that includes evenings, weekends and sleep-ins.
Vacancy Reference Number: 83469
Applications for this role must be submitted via the Creative Support website using the above vacancy reference number.

Why Creative Support?
Creative Support is a passionate, inclusive, and anti-racist organisation. We are a Stonewall Diversity Champion, Disability Confident Employer who have recently received Investors in People Silver award. We actively encourage applications from candidates from all backgrounds and cultures, however we can only accept applications from candidates who are located in and eligible to work within the UK. We would advise all applicants to read our job application privacy notice which can be found on the Creative Support website.
summary: Recovery Support Worker role in Bradford focused on mental health support.

title: Crisis Support Worker
description:
Do you have the commitment and positive values to make a difference to the lives of people living in Bradford?

As a Crisis Support Worker you will be providing person centred recovery focused support to adults with mental health needs accessing our crisis service in Shipley, Bradford. You will work with service users to develop a recovery support plan based on their presenting needs and preferences to help them achieve their goals and aspirations. It is expected that you can support and mentor new staff and provide excellent customer service to service users and liaise effectively with local statutory and voluntary agencies.
You will be required to communicate with service users, family members, housing and other staff to ensure a consistent and coordinated service. You will work creatively and effectively with individuals of complex backgrounds and communication styles and may have a variety of support needs, including mental health issues, substance misuse, self-harm and hoarding.
This role would suit highly motivated, self-starters with previous crisis support experience, excellent communication skills and a calm, resourceful and energetic approach to person centred support. Senior and supervisory experience is desirable. Support Worker Duties:

- Building and maintaining trusting and meaningful relationships with service users,

- Carrying out general administrative duties,

- Responding to crisis and emergency situations,

- Developing and sustaining warm and trusting relationships with service users,

- Carrying out assessments and creating and updating support plans,

- Collaborating with service users, family members and other involved professionals to provide a consistent and coordinated support service,

- Providing support and supervision to staff in all aspects of their role,

- Maintaining a safe, calm and clean environment.

For all our Relief roles, previous care experience is essential. As a Creative Support Relief Support Worker staff member you will have access to our free app, which allows you to pick up shifts in any area of the country and provides information on the service and shifts, including directions.
As a Creative Support Bank Staff member you will have access to our free app, which allows you to pick up shifts in any area of the country and provides information on the service and shifts, including directions.

Vacancy Reference Number: 66829
Applications for this role must be submitted via the Creative Support website using the above vacancy reference number.

Benefits of working with Creative Support:

- Free 24/7 counselling and employee support programme,

- Choice of weekly or monthly pay,

- Competitive pay and a pension with company contribution and 28 days annual leave,

- Company paid enhanced DBS for all staff,

- Additional bonus on completion of the Care Certificate.
summary: Crisis Support Worker role in Bradford focused on mental health support.

title: Support Worker
description:
Support Worker

Location:
Dewsbury

Pay:
£13.86 -£14.61 per hour

Work Patterns
- You will work rewarding long day shifts including early starts, late evenings, weekends and sleep in's fairly planned within the rotas.

You will need to have the Right to Work in the UK. Sponsorship is not available for this position

About us
At our West Yorkshire Supported Living Services in Dewsbury, we support people with learning disabilities and physical disabilities to live the lives they choose. We support adults from the age of 18 to move into their own homes and to live independently. They each have their own self-contained apartments, and there are Hub communal areas where they can meet with friends and family, and access support when they need it. We offer support around the clock, and some people may also receive support in the community.
As a support worker, your role is to provide person centred support to people with a learning disability, helping them to live independent and fulfilling lives. The support we offer is tailored to the individual client and can include support with personal care, medication, social activities and accessing the local community.
You will be joining a diverse and inclusive team who are passionate about helping people to progress and grow.

Role Responsibility

- Being a support worker is a role where you can make every day matter. It's a job with challenges that make your heart beat faster, where you can give something of yourself and empower amazing people on their journey.

- We know that life of a support worker can be hard and emotionally challenging, but with a positive attitude, patience, resilience and determination all our staff can make a difference and support people on their journey to the lives they're hoping to lead.

- We can only accept Support Workers who have a Full UK driving licence due to the needs of the people we support.

The Ideal Candidate

- Passionate, caring and empathetic.

- Flexible and have a desire to support people to achieve their goals.

- Can work independently and as part of a team.

- Have a positive attitude and be resilient.

- Have good communication skills.

- Have a full UK driving licence.

Benefits

We offer all support workers:

- Affinity Trust Wagestream app that enables you to track and receive your earned pay instantly and save towards your financial goals, and discounts cards for food, clothing, toys and days out.

- Full training including the opportunity to complete the Care Certificate, and Diploma in Health & Social Care.

- E-learning webinars.

- Access to specialist care sector training, all fully paid for by us.

- Loads of career progression opportunities.

- We are a Living Wage Employer.

- Refer a friend scheme, be rewarded for recommending a friend to work with us.

- Employee welfare and wellbeing initiatives.

- Recognition schemes, team events and social activities.

- Employee discount schemes across a range of retailers and services.

- Staff incentive schemes such as Refer a Friend, Employee of the Month and Heroes Awards.

- A supportive and friendly team that is committed to making a difference.
summary: Support Worker role in Dewsbury focused on support for adults with learning disabilities.

title: SEN Support Workers
description:
SEN Support Workers Needed - Halifax & Huddersfield - Immediate Starts & September Roles!

Are you passionate about supporting children with additional needs? Do you have experience with SEN - whether through work, education, or personal/family life? If so, we want to hear from you!

At , we are urgently recruiting SEN Support Workers for a range of roles across Halifax and Huddersfield. Opportunities are available for immediate starts, as well as positions beginning in September. We work closely with a variety of schools and specialist provisions, matching the right candidates to the right settings.

These roles are ideal for individuals with:

- Experience working in schools, care, youth work, support work or similar settings

- Personal or family experience supporting children with additional needs

- A calm, patient and nurturing approach

- A passion for helping children thrive both academically and emotionally

Responsibilities may include:

- Providing 1:1 or small group support for children with SEN

- Assisting with emotional regulation, communication and behaviour strategies

- Supporting children with autism, ADHD, SEMH or complex needs

- Working closely with teachers and pastoral teams to ensure each child is fully supported

What we offer:

- Flexible roles to suit your availability

- Opportunities for both temporary and long-term positions

- Supportive schools and specialist settings

- Ongoing guidance from an experienced education recruitment team

Whether you're an experienced SEN professional or looking to start a career in support work, we'd love to help you take the next step.

Apply today to be considered for immediate or upcoming roles in Halifax and Huddersfield.

Huntress does not discriminate on the grounds of age, race, gender, disability, creed or sexual orientation and complies with all relevant UK legislation. NextGen Teachers is a wholly owned subsidiary of Huntress.
summary: SEN Support Workers role in Halifax focused on SEN support in education settings.

title: Relief Support Worker
description:
We are seeking warm, motivated, and person-centred Relief Support Workers to join our team in Wakefield. You will be providing personalised care and support to adults with learning disabilities, each with unique support needs. This role requires promoting active and independent lifestyles in line with the principles of Creative Support.

In this role, you will offer respectful personal care and practical and emotional support, helping service users engage in social activities and maintain connections with family and friends. You will support service users in all aspects of everyday living, using positive behaviour support and autism-specific approaches to create consistency and structure in their lives. You must be willing to participate in day trips, community activities, and to provide support with domestic tasks and administration.
No two days will be the same, and you will have the opportunity to learn and work in a supportive environment. You will need to be able to travel within the local area, and you should have either access to your own vehicle or be able to use public transport to get to the service. We are looking for candidates from all walks of life. No previous care experience is needed, as full training and support will be provided. As long as you are reliable, motivated, and have a can-do attitude, you can apply your existing skills to this role. We do, however, look for people with a real passion for person-centred care and support.

Applications for this role must be submitted via the Creative Support website using the vacancy reference number 81727. Full training is provided, as is the opportunity to work towards QCF Diploma in Health and Social Care. As a Creative Support employee, you will have access to our free app, allowing you to pick up shifts in any area of the country and providing information about the service, including directions.
You will be paid weekly.
Creative Support is a passionate, inclusive, and anti-racist organization. We are a Stonewall Diversity Champion, Disability Confident Employer, and have recently received the Investors in People Silver award. We actively encourage applications from candidates from all backgrounds and cultures. However, we can only accept applications from candidates who are located in and eligible to work within the UK. We would advise all applicants to read our job application privacy notice, which is available on the Creative Support website.
summary: Relief Support Worker role in Wakefield focused on support for adults with learning disabilities.

title: Support Worker
description:
We are looking for compassionate Support Workers to empower adults with learning disabilities to lead independent and fulfilling lives in Wakefield. We believe in recognizing and nurturing our clients' strengths, abilities, and personal resources.

Join us in providing personalised care and support, tailoring your approach to meet each individual's unique needs. We need warm, motivated and person-centred individuals with a keen interest in promoting active and independent lifestyles. No two days will be the same as you support individuals in all aspects of their daily lives and identify opportunities for our service users to engage in exciting social activities both within and outside their homes. Your role will include:

- Providing support with personal care and domestic tasks and encouraging service users to exercise choice and control over their lives,

- Collaborating with service users, their families and involved professionals to provide a coordinated and consistent service,

- Assisting service users with accessing and participating in their local community in Wakefield.

Wakefield enjoys excellent public transport links to neighbouring towns and cities and is just over 15 minutes from Leeds by train. Creative Support prides itself on offering its employees a comprehensive range of benefits and rewards. You will benefit from all our Creative Support bonus rewards such as Wagestream, a fully paid DBS and generous company pension provided by Penfold, as well as a health cash plan to claim towards dental, glasses, therapy etc, and free access to round the clock employee assistance programme. As a Creative Support Bank Staff member you will have access to our free app, allowing you to pick up shifts in any area of the country and providing information about the service, including directions.
Applications for this role must be submitted via the Creative Support website using the vacancy reference number 81686.
Benefits of working with Creative Support:

- Free 24/7 counselling and employee support programme,

- Choice of weekly or monthly pay,

- Competitive pay and a pension with company contribution and 28 days annual leave,

- Company paid enhanced DBS for all staff,

- Additional bonus on completion of the Care Certificate.
summary: Support Worker role in Wakefield focused on support for adults with learning disabilities.
